item.title: WA Concealed Carry Class at Wildlife Committee of
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Wildlife Committee of Washington - WCWShootingSports - Kenmore Range, 1031 228th St SW, Bothell, WA

This 4-hour comprehensive training course is designed to educate individuals on the laws and regulations surrounding the carrying of concealed firearms. This class covers essential topics such as the use of force, firearms safety, and the proper handling and storage of firearms, as well as the specific laws and any requirements for obtaining permits in Washington, Oregon, Arizona, and Utah. Upon completion of the class, attendees will be eligible to apply for the above-mentioned permits. You will also receive a WA - HB-1143 Gun Safety Certificate. DATES and TIMES are subject to change, please view our website for the most up to date and accurate class info. https://www.legalheat.com/classes/in-person/concealed-carry-class-at-wildlife-committee-of-washington-bothell-wa/?utm\_source=facebook&utm\_medium=event

item.title: Intro to Improv Eastside - Issaquah SUNDAYS
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Discover improv in Issaquah. Build confidence, meet new people, and learn to create stories through fun, supportive games and scene work. Looking for a fun, fresh way to connect with others, spark creativity, and step out of the everyday routine? Welcome to Intro to Improv at Unexpected Productions Improv School Eastside in Issaquah. Whether you are a working professional hoping to improve communication, a creative soul searching for a new outlet, or someone simply wanting more laughter and community in your week, this class is the perfect place to begin. In this beginner-friendly course, you will learn the foundational skills of improvisation: active listening, collaboration, spontaneity, and using your own life experiences to create honest, engaging scenes and stories. Improv is not about being funny, it is about being present, supportive, and open. And yes, along the way, you will laugh more than you have in ages. Our community values connection and growth, and this class is built on those principles. You will work in a supportive group where the guiding philosophy is simple: When your scene partner looks good, you look good. Together, we build trust, confidence, and creativity, one scene at a time. Under the guidance of our experienced faculty, you will learn to: Be a better listener Conquer fears and build confidence Spark creativity Meet new people and make real connections Become a better problem solver Say yes more in your life Step out of your comfort zone Improve public speaking Fail easier \(and laugh about it\) Discover more about yourself Have an absolute blast This class includes lots of laughter, lots of stage time, and zero pressure. Every student begins here. No experience needed. Just bring curiosity and a willingness to try something new. Unexpected Productions Improv School is thrilled to bring our newest Eastside improv classes to Issaquah, serving the communities of the Sammamish Plateau, East Renton, Bellevue, Snoqualmie Valley, and beyond. Class Details Dates: Sundays for eight \(8\) weeks, from March 8 to May 3, \(No class on April 5th\) Time: 11:00 AM to 1:00pm Location: Improv School Eastside Issaquah 1315 NW Mall Street, Suite 4 Issaquah, WA 98027 \(next to the Gaslamp Bar & Grill\) Maximum students: 16 Ages: 18 plus Cost: $320 Prerequisite: None Note: Attendance at the first session is mandatory, as it sets the tone and foundation for the entire quarter. If you know you will miss the first class, please register for a future session. What students say “You are learning more about improv, more about yourself, making interesting friends, while you are learning and stretching in ways that will make life better both at work and socially, yet everyone is smiling so hard your face hurts. How many stars is that worth?” Jonathan A., 5 Stars on Yelp Cancellation policy There are no refunds.

item.title: Food and Cooking Through Science
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Gain a deeper insight into what’s going on when you cook and bake! Whether you are a home cook or a professional, you can become a much better cook by learning the science of food and cooking. You'll fall in love with cooking, as well as the beauty of the amazing world we live in. Focus on understanding the fascinating field of food science through a combination of fun and fascinating lectures and demos. Learn to make better choices about cooking methods and techniques, and how to produce better finished products more easily and consistently. Elevate your knowledge about nutrition, how food works, sustainability issues, and the chemistry of cooking and baking. Develop modern techniques using scientific principles. Learn many fundamental concepts and apply them to real-world cooking and baking.

item.title: Winter Improv Student Showcase
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Come see the improv stars of the future debut the skills they’ve learned in Unexpected Productions’ Improv School. Join us tonight at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater for the Winter Student Showcase! Watch as our talented students—ranging from beginner to advanced levels—take the stage to craft spontaneous, engaging, and often hilarious improv scenes right before your eyes. Our 300, 400, and 500-level students will showcase their newly honed skills, giving you an exclusive preview of the next generation of improv stars. You’ll experience a variety of performance styles inspired by UP’s mainstage shows, making for an unpredictable and highly entertaining night. Stick around after the show to meet the performers—you’ll be able to say, “I saw them before they were famous!” 6:00pm: Chad Sunday 300, Bestock 400, Tim 400, Tony 500 8:00pm: Chad Monday 300, Chazz 400, Laurel 400, Liz 500 Don’t miss this chance to support our rising improv stars and enjoy an evening of laughter and surprises. See you there!

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: Coworking Tuesdays
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Bothell Kenmore Chamber, 10031 Main Street, Suite A, Bothell, WA

Tuesday Cowork \+ Coffee/Tea \(Weekly\) Need a reason to get out of your inbox cave and into a productive, low-pressure space? Join us every Tuesday for a drop-in coworking morning—bring your laptop, knock out your priorities, and enjoy the energy of working alongside other local professionals. When: Tuesdays | 9:30–11:30 AM | No registration needed Format: Come for the full two hours or pop in when you can Who it’s for: Business owners, remote workers, creatives, students, nonprofit leaders—anyone who wants focused work time \+ light community What to expect: Quiet “get-it-done” work blocks, optional hellos \+ quick intros, and a friendly vibe \(no forced networking\) Location \(through February\): Bothell Visitor Center We’ll rotate locations monthly—same time, same Tuesday rhythm, fresh spaces. Bring: Your laptop \+ whatever you need to work. We have the coffee, tea, and water! Questions? Email sandra@bothellkenmorechamber.org

item.title: Tea with T: Informational Webinar on WEBS
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Description Tea with T: How to Use WEBS Tuesday, March 10 | 10:30 AM PT | Zoom Join GSBA for Tea with T, an informational webinar led by Toraya Miller, where we'll walk through Washington's Electronic Business Solution \(WEBS\) and how to register and use it to find state contracting opportunities. Bring a cup of tea and get set up in 5-10 minutes so bid opportunities that match your commodity codes can start coming straight to your inbox. What is WEBS? Washington's Electronic Business Solution \(WEBS\) is an internet-based bid notification system hosted by the Department of Enterprise Services \(DES\). Many state agencies and educational institutions use WEBS to notify businesses of contracting opportunities based on the commodity codes you select. About Toraya Miller Director of Training & Consulting, GSBA Toraya Miller leads GSBA's training and consulting work, supporting businesses across Washington as they navigate growth, certification, and public-sector opportunities. In this session, Toraya will guide participants through WEBS and share practical tips for getting registered, staying updated, and using the system to identify contracting opportunities with the state. In this webinar, we'll cover: ☕ What WEBS is and why it matters for vendors ☕ How to register as a vendor in WEBS ☕ How to choose and update your commodity codes ☕ Where to find current and upcoming bid opportunities ☕ How to use forecasted purchases to plan ahead ☕ Tips to keep your profile updated so opportunities keep brewing Learn more about WEBS Washington Department of Enterprise Services: Bid Opportunities \+ WEBS

item.title: Get Grant Ready
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: GSBA, 400 E Pine St Ste 322, Seattle, WA

Description Get Grant Ready: Grants Preparedness Workshop for Micro Businesses Join GSBA for a practical Spring workshop focused on helping small and micro businesses break down barriers to securing grants. About This Workshop Facilitated by GSBA's Grant Writer & Manager, Devonshire Lokke, this Spring workshop will focus on breaking down barriers to securing grants for small and micro business. Topics will include grant research \(where to find grants for small businesses\), grant writing \(how to apply for grants\), budgeting \(how to track and manage grant funds\), and reporting \(how to share successes and challenges with funders\). As a grant writer and manager for 6\+ years, Devonshire knows that grants can seem intimidating and overwhelming, especially for a small or micro-business owner. This workshop will provide entrepreneurs with the confidence, tools, and resources to quickly and competitively pursue future grant opportunities \(presented by both GSBA and other funders\). Workshops will be open to GSBA members and non-members and will be in person at the GSBA offices in Capitol Hill, Seattle. The session will conclude with 30 minutes of Q&A, to address participants' unique business needs. What You'll Learn Where to find grant opportunities relevant to small and micro businesses How to read and assess grant requirements before applying Best practices for grant applications and narratives How to build and track grant budgets with confidence What funders expect in grant reporting and follow-up Common pitfalls-and how to avoid them Who Should Attend Micro businesses and sole proprietors Early-stage entrepreneurs Businesses applying for grants for the first time Businesses that have applied before but want stronger results Business owners seeking clear, practical guidance No prior grant experience is required.

Event details

item.title: SafeCampus Violence Prevention and Response Training
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: University of Washington, Seattle, WA

Thursday, Mar 12, 2026, 9:30 - 11 a.m. PDT During this interactive training, we ask participants to consider their unique role in preventing violence at the University of Washington. This training has been adapted to consider the remote academic environments that many staff, students, and faculty are currently experiencing. We will explore mental health and warning signs of different forms of violence including; workplace violence, relationship violence, sexual harassment, and stalking. We will briefly practice in-person and remote bystander intervention techniques and build your understanding of UW resources. Click the link below to register for this training. Campus room: Online Zoom Training Accessibility Contact: safecampus@uw.edu Event Types: Information Sessions, Lectures/Seminars, Workshops More info: forms.office.com…

item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Join fellow Seattle University Albers School Marketing alumni for an engaging virtual conversation designed to strengthen connections and support your continued professional growth. This interactive session will feature a facilitated Q&A with industry leaders Katie Milburn \(’14\), Manager of Internal Recruiting at Salesforce, and Lindsey Davis, Senior Manager of Strategy and Operations, People eXperience and Technology \(PXT\) at AWS. Together, they will share insights on career development, leadership, navigating change, and building meaningful professional pathways in today’s evolving workplace. Following the moderated discussion, participants will move into small breakout rooms for more personalized conversations with industry mentors and Albers faculty. This format offers a unique opportunity to ask questions, exchange ideas, and connect with peers in a more informal setting. Whether you are early in your career, considering your next move, or looking to expand your professional network, this event is designed to provide practical advice, fresh perspectives, and renewed connection to the Albers Marketing community. We look forward to seeing you online!

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Monthly Native Plant Nursery Work Party Calling all native plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nursery. Every year we host a c ouple thousand native plants in the nursery until they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great oppor tunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted plants, up-potting or repotting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, and watering. When: Thursday, March 12th, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants, boots or sneakers\). item.title: Lushootseed Speaker Series: Natosha Gobin
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Lemieux Library, Byte Cafe, Second Floor, Seattle, WA

RSVP and join us for a special gathering as we welcome Natosha Gobin \(ɬalulic̓aʔ II\), a Tulalip Tribal member and descendant of the Snohomish, Snoqualmie, Sauk-Suiattle, Skagit, and Puyallup peoples. Natosha's lineage connects her to the Gobin, Young, Price, Hill, Jones, and Williams families. She brings her connection to family into her roles as a proud mother, auntie, and longtime community educator. For over 25 years, Natosha has taught, spoken, and revitalized Northern Lushootseed through the Tulalip Tribes Lushootseed Department, where she now serves as manager. Across her teaching journey, she has worked with all ages and currently co-teaches courses at Marysville-Pilchuck High School, Northwest Indian College, and the University of Washington. Open to the public at no cost, this event offers an opportunity to hear storytelling rooted in Natosha’s ancestral background and extensive knowledge of the Northern Lushootseed language. Early registration is encouraged, as space and tickets are limited. Co-sponsored with the Lemieux Library & McGoldrick Learning Center.

Event details

item.title: 5th Annual Epoch Symposium: Power of Defiance in the Face of Law
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 8 AM

5th Annual Epoch Symposium: Power of Defiance in the Face of Law Presented by Seattle University School of Law, the Black Law Student Association, and the Law Review Date: Friday, March 13, 2026 Time: 8:00 AM – 1:30 PM PT \(11:00 AM – 4:30 PM ET\) Location: Virtual \(via Zoom\) Join us for the 5th Annual Epoch Symposium, exploring this year’s powerful theme: Power of Defiance in the Face of Law. Our distinguished scholars will examine how Black and Brown communities have historically radicalized the law through acts of resistance. Discussions will explore how legal structures continue to sustain systems of power, and crucially, how the law itself can be reclaimed as an instrument of defiance. The program features prominent critical race scholars and practitioners from across the nation engaging in timely discussions on the history of law as both a site of oppression and a tool of defiance. Featured Speakers & Highlights Institutional Welcome: Kristin DiBiase, Associate Dean for Student Affairs Opening Remarks: Professor Richard Delgado & Professor Jean Stefancic Keynote Address: Karen Koehler, nationally recognized civil rights and personal injury litigator and partner at The Stritmatter Firm. Special Feature: A livestream podcast recording of The Velvet Hammer, discussing the legal landscape of current events. Closing Remarks: Elinor Jones Toutant, on behalf of BLSA. Symposium Schedule Note: Times listed are in Pacific Time \(PT\) 8:00 AM: Institutional Welcome: Kristin DiBiase, Associate Dean for Student Affairs 8:05 AM: Opening Remarks by Prof. Richard Delgado & Prof. Jean Stefancic 8:20 AM: Panel 1 9:25 AM: Panel 2 10:30 AM: Break 11:00 AM: Keynote Address by Karen Koehler \(with Q&A\) 12:00 PM: Podcast Recording of The Velvet Hammer 12:30 PM: Panel 3 1:30 PM: Closing Remarks by Elinor Jones Toutant The Zoom link for this event will be sent to RSVP's a few days before the event. If you register for the event on or after March 11th, please reach out to Anita Jahangiri \(ajahangiri@seattleu.edu\) to receive the link.

item.title: Lichen Walk
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Dr. Katherine Glew. Have you always wondered what lichens are and why they are found on your trees, rhododendrons and azaleas? Lichens are harmless to your plants and add aesthetic value to your garden. Learn about common lichens found in an urban environment and what they tell us about air quality, forest health, geology and why they are indicators of changes in the climate, ecosystems, and geology. You will leave with a fresh understanding about lichens and a user-friendly chart that lists lichens found in your neighborhood. This walk will be on trails with various surfaces and may include gravel, mulch, or pavement. Please dress for the weather and wear comfortable walking shoes. $28 BBGS members/$40 nonmembers.

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session\\\*\*\* Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate \(paper, t-shirts, etc\) - Water based ink Vera \*does not\* allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. \*\*FAQ\*\* 1. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

item.title: Introduction to Orchid Care and Repotting
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Volunteer Park Conservatory, 1400 East Galer Street, Seattle, WA

Introduction to Orchids and their care. The orchid family \(Orchidacea\) is one of the largest and most diverse plant families. It possibly represents 10% of the flowering plant species. There are between 20,000 and 35,000 species \(about 800 genera\) and can be found on every continent except Antarctica. There are 41 native terrestrial orchid species in Washington State. Orchids are characterized by having highly specialized flower adaptations usually for pollination by insects. This class will cover the physical characteristics of orchids, care and feeding of the plants. Each student is encourages to bring up to 3 of their plants for evaluation and possible repotting. The lecture and demonstration portion of the class will be one hour followed by the evaluation and repotting demonstrations for an hour.

item.title: Inside the Clerkship
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Sullivan Hall, 110, Seattle, WA

Join us for an exclusive and candid panel discussion featuring distinguished judges and former and current law clerks who will demystify the clerkship application process. Designed for law students at all stages, this event offers firsthand insight into what judges look for in applicants, how to stand out in a competitive pool, and how to strategically prepare throughout law school. Panelists will share practical advice on building strong relationships with faculty, crafting compelling application materials, navigating interviews, and positioning yourself for both state and federal clerkships. Attendees will also gain valuable perspective on what life is like inside chambers and how a clerkship can shape long-term career trajectories. In addition, this program will preview the Federal Litigation course offered in Fall 2026. Attendees will learn how the course strengthens the research, writing, and advocacy skills that judges value most in clerkship candidates, and how it can position students for success in competitive judicial hiring. Whether you're just beginning to consider a clerkship or preparing to apply, this program will provide actionable guidance, insider tips, and an opportunity to ask questions directly to those involved in the hiring process—while offering a forward-looking glimpse at how the upcoming Federal Litigation course can support your goals. Panelists: Judge Roopali Desai, United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit Judge Kymberly Evanson, United States District Court for the Western District of Washington Judge Jamal Whitehead, United States District Court for the Western District of Washington Luther Reed-Caulkins, Current Judicial Law Clerk to the Honorable Kymberly Evanson Ruby Nagamine, Current Judicial Law Clerk to the Honorable Richard A. Jones, United States District Court for the Western District of Washington Dalvin Yarbrough, Former Judicial Law Clerk for The Honorable Reggie B. Walton, United States District Court for the District of Columbia, 2023-2025 Moderator: Cynthia Jones, Jones Legal Group and Adjunct Professor, Seattle University School of Law \(Federal Litigation\) Date: March 16th, 2026 Time: 4:00 PM to 5:30 PM Location: Sullivan Hall, Room 110

item.title: Food and Cooking Through Science
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Gain a deeper insight into what’s going on when you cook and bake! Whether you are a home cook or a professional, you can become a much better cook by learning the science of food and cooking. You'll fall in love with cooking, as well as the beauty of the amazing world we live in. Focus on understanding the fascinating field of food science through a combination of fun and fascinating lectures and demos. Learn to make better choices about cooking methods and techniques, and how to produce better finished products more easily and consistently. Elevate your knowledge about nutrition, how food works, sustainability issues, and the chemistry of cooking and baking. Develop modern techniques using scientific principles. Learn many fundamental concepts and apply them to real-world cooking and baking.

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.description:

item.title: Working Together to Protect and Transfer Assets - VIRTUAL WEBINAR
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 11 AM

Description ️ Working Together to Protect and Transfer Assets Presented by: Peggy Haslach & Patrice Kreider-Hughes Location: GSBA Office Date & Time: March 18 11am - 12:30pm Join GSBA for an informative session designed to help LGBTQ\+ individuals, couples, and families protect what matters most. Working Together to Protect and Transfer Assets brings together a financial planner and an estate planning attorney to show how collaboration ensures your wishes are honored, your assets are protected, and your loved ones are cared for. What You'll Learn How to build a trusted planning team that understands LGBTQ\+ financial and legal needs Strategies to protect assets during your lifetime with coordinated insurance, investments, and legal planning Essential documents: durable powers of attorney, healthcare directives, wills, and trusts Aligning beneficiary designations with legal structures for seamless asset transfer Best practices across diverse family structures and varying state laws Featured Speakers Peggy Haslach, CFP, CLU Financial Planner working with women professionals and small business owners Peggy built her practice serving women professionals \(including veterinarians, doctors, and attorneys\) and small business owners. After seeing first-hand how life events impact women's financial security, she pivoted careers at 53-focused on collaborative, educational planning for women and LGBTQ\+ clients. Her clients are forward-thinking women who've put the pieces together-business, life, goals, beliefs, and savings-but want a cohesive plan they fully understand. Outside of work, Peggy competes nationally in dog agility and shares that passion with her wife, Desiree, a national and world champion handler and trainer. They live in Sumner, Washington with their three Border Collies-Vista, Cru, and Peak. Patrice M. Kreider-Hughes, Esq. Founder & Principal, Kreider Hughes Law, PLLC Patrice is an estate planning and small-business attorney with 15\+ years of experience. She earned her B.A. in psychology from the University of Virginia and her J.D. from Seattle University School of Law. Before founding Kreider Hughes Law in 2017, she served as in-house counsel for Conover Insurance, handling mergers and acquisitions, contracts, and employment matters. Featured on the Seattle Connected podcast and recognized by Expertise.com in 2023 and 2024, Patrice lives in Seattle with her wife, their daughter Simone, and pets Luther and Emma. Session Outline Why Collaboration Matters Financial planners and estate attorneys working in tandem to build resilient, values-aligned plans. Building Your Planning Team Roles, responsibilities, and coordination between advisors who understand LGBTQ\+ needs. Protecting Assets During Your Lifetime Insurance \(life, disability, long-term care\), investment strategy \(diversification, ESG\), and ownership/titling alignment. Legal Documents That Safeguard Your Wishes Durable POA, healthcare POA & advance directive, wills & trusts, and syncing beneficiary designations. Coordinating Asset Transfers Real estate titling, retirement & insurance beneficiaries, business succession, and philanthropic giving. Navigating Legal Landscapes & Family Structures Marriage recognition, parental rights, cross-border/visa considerations, and state-by-state variability. Q&A \(15 min\) - Bring your questions on real estate, trusts, chosen family, and children's inheritance planning. Who Should Attend LGBTQ\+ individuals, couples, and families seeking long-term financial security Small business owners planning for succession and legacy Anyone looking to align legal and financial strategies

item.title: Immigration is Not Invasion: Dean's Luminaries in Law Series with Ilya Somin
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Sullivan Hall, C5, Seattle, WA

Seattle University School of Law’s Luminaries in Law Series continues with a timely and thought-provoking conversation titled Immigration Is Not Invasion, featuring renowned constitutional law scholar Ilya Somin. In this special lunchtime event, Professor Somin will be interviewed by Anthony E. Varona, Dean of Seattle University School of Law, and Andrew Siegel, Professor of Law. Together, they will explore Professor Somin’s scholarship challenging the increasingly common framing of immigration as an “invasion,” examining its constitutional, historical, and democratic implications. The discussion will engage questions at the intersection of immigration law, constitutional structure, political rhetoric, and civil liberties, offering insight into how legal doctrine and public discourse shape immigration policy and democratic values. Drawing on their respective expertise, Dean Varona and Professor Siegel will guide a wide-ranging conversation designed to illuminate one of the most pressing legal and societal debates of our time. Students, faculty, and members of the broader legal community are invited to attend this engaging installment of the Luminaries in Law series. Date: March 19th Location: Room C5, Sullivan Hall Time: 12:00 PM to 12:50 PM.

Event details

item.title: Free Virtual Sessions for Adoption
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Northwest Adoption Exchange \(NWAE\) is inviting all those interested in adopting from foster care to attend free, virtual information sessions to learn about the process. Offered in partnership with the Washington State Department of Children, Youth, and Families \(DCYF\), these sessions provide guidance and clarity for anyone beginning or continuing their adoption journey. Who: Open to anyone interested in adopting from foster care in Washington state. What: Free virtual information sessions on the adoption process. When: Monthly sessions, participants are encouraged to attend Session 1 before attending Session 2.  Upcoming dates: ·         Session 1: March 5 at 12 p.m. or 6 p.m. ·         Session 2: March 19 at 12 p.m. or 6 p.m. Where: Online, register at nwae.org Cost: Free Led by experienced child welfare professionals, NWAE’s sessions are presented virtually in two parts and offer a comprehensive introduction to adoption. Participants will gain insights into the needs of youth waiting for permanent families and learn how to navigate each stage of the adoption journey with confidence. Session 1: Introduction to adoption from foster care Explore how adoption fits into the foster care system, the types of support available, and what to consider when determining if adoption is right for your family. Session 2: The steps to adoption Walk through the practical aspects of adoption, from choosing an agency and completing required training to navigating the home study process.

Event details

item.title: Albers Graduate Information Session
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Interested in a graduate program at Albers? Have questions about the admissions process? Want to connect with a current student or faculty member from your program of interest? Our online information sessions provide more insight into your desired program and answer any questions you may have. Join us at one of our events to learn more! During our graduate information sessions we take a deep dive into the following programs offered by the Albers School of Business and Economics: Early Career MBAProfessional MBA \(in-person program only\)Sport and Entertainment Management MBAMaster of Professional Accounting \(STEM, for non-accounting majors\)Master of Science in Accounting & Analytics \(STEM, for accounting majors\)Master of Science in Business Analytics \(in-person program only\) \(STEM\)Master of Science in Finance \(STEM\) We provide detailed information such as program structure, prerequisites, application information, admissions processes, scholarships, and more. To participate with video, you will need access to a device with a camera \(laptop, smart phone, etc.\). A Zoom account is not required. You may also choose to participate through audio only. Albers has separate recruiting events for the following: Online: MBA, MSBA, Cybersecurity Leadership programs Leadership Executive programs including the MBA and Certificate Please email albersgradinfo@seattleu.edu for more information.

item.title: Women's Conference
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Join us for a day centered on Jesus, our wonderful counselor. We’ll hear from Kacey Batterton, a licensed counselor, sharing how the care Jesus modeled in scripture is echoed in how counselors are taught to care for others. Together, we’ll explore what Christlike empathy, listening, and compassion can look like for all of us. Not as experts, but as women learning to love well. This is a free, one-day event open to women of all ages \(teens 13\+ welcome\). Bring a friend, everyone is invited! Lunch isn’t included, but we’ll have a room reserved at a local restaurant for anyone who’d like to gather during the break. Come as you are. Be encouraged, equipped, and reminded of the gentle care of Jesus.

item.title: Best Day Ever: South Park
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: South Park Hall, 1253 S Cloverdale St., Seattle, WA

Join us for our first Best Day Ever event with Cultivate South Park! This exciting one-day event will feature activities like West African dance & storytelling, chocolate tasting, Duwamish River tours, local food tasting, arts & crafts, and more. Sign up now for a full day of joy and community! Best Day Ever is a one-day, community-powered experience that celebrates the vibrancy, culture, and pride of Seattle’s neighborhoods. Co-created by the Department of Neighborhoods \(DON\) and the Seattle Chamber of Connection \(SCoC\), Best Day Ever strengthens civic belonging by bringing people, place, and purpose together. Guided by DON’s Community Engagement Framework and the Chamber’s Six Points of Connection, this initiative uplifts community leadership and honors the histories, creativity, and cultural assets that make each neighborhood unique. A thriving neighborhood is one where residents feel connected, represented, and proud of where they live — and Best Day Ever is designed to bring that feeling to life. Each activation builds belonging through service, storytelling, movement, shared meals, joyful connection, and economic visibility for local small businesses.

Event details

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop [VIRTUAL]
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   Virtual workshops are held every 3rd and 4th Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. A Zoom link will be sent to you via a ULMS Housing department staff member upon completing registration.

Event details

item.title: BrainFest with Allen Institute
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pacific Science Center, 200 Second Ave. North, Seattle, WA

Join us for the third annual BrainFest, a day of brainy fun for all ages at PacSci. Explore hands-on activities, meet Allen Institute scientists and collaborators, learn about cutting-edge neuroscience research, and even touch a real brain! Whether you’re a budding young scientist, a lifelong learner, or simply fascinated by how the mind works, BrainFest 2026 promises a full day of discovery, inspiration, and wonder. Tickets BrainFest is included with General Admission. A limited number of free general admission tickets will be given out on the day of the event on a first-come first-serve basis. This event is a partnership of the Allen Institute and Pacific Science Center.
